findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 32. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements - the outer <main> wraps the content, and there is a second <main> inside it. This is invalid HTML structure but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> as the outermost wrapper (from the template), then <header> followed by another <main> element containing the Q&A content.

findings.description: The 'Background' section is rendered as h2 but in the PDF it appears as a bold/underlined section heading. This is acceptable structural interpretation, but the Q&A items lack any sub-heading structure - all 20 questions are at the same flat level with no heading tags, which matches the PDF's flat structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows 'Background' as an underlined heading; HTML uses

Background

. All Q&A items are rendered as paragraphs with bold labels, matching the PDF structure.

findings.description: The page footers ('LASII Stormwater Program Questions and Answers / LASII Program Administered by NC Dept. of Environmental Quality Division of Water Infrastructure / Page X') that appear on each page of the PDF are correctly omitted from the HTML body content, which is acceptable.
findings.evidence: Footer text appears on all 5 PDF pages but is not reproduced in the HTML content, which is expected behavior.

findings.description: In Answer 3, the PDF has a typo 'ANSWSER' which the HTML faithfully preserves. This is not a conversion error but confirms text fidelity.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'ANSWSER: No.' HTML: 'ANSWSER: No.' - both contain the same typo.
